action.reasoning: Observation: Best test PESQ stuck at 3.137 (MetricGAN+ w/ fixed -25 LUFS norm; val PESQ flat at 2.862 across all post-proc/hybrids). ConvTasNet (~1-5M params after capacity increases) plateaus ~2.02 val PESQ even after 30 epochs (train loss improves to ~-14 but insufficient recovery). test_enhance.py confirms MG superiority; train.py val loop incomplete/buggy in places; no model beats baseline yet. predict.py/test_enhance.py are mature for offline MetricGAN load + norm + /tmp/enhanced. Analysis: MetricGAN+ is task-specific (pretrained directly on VoiceBank+DEMAND to optimize PESQ), explaining why generic from-scratch ConvTasNet struggles in limited epochs/iterations. Post-processing and hybrids saturated (flat curves). With 3.3h/6 iters left, risky to commit more training runs that may not surpass 3.137 before budget exhaustion (each full train+test_enhance ~3-5min). Decision: Finalize with strongest config - write clean self-contained predict.py (uses speechbrain.inference.SpectralMaskEnhancement from metricgan_checkpoint, -25 LUFS via pyloudnorm, saves 16kHz mono WAVs to /tmp/enhanced per exact eval contract, handles args). Run test_enhance.py for confirmation, then submit_eval on metricgan_checkpoint to lock best score. No more ConvTasNet/HDemucs attempts to conserve budget.
